label: Button
purpose: Maps a variant prop to the two existing CTA button classes in homepage.css. Renders as <a> when href is provided, <button> otherwise. Introduces no new CSS.

props.name: label
props.description: Button text. No HTML — plain string only. Button has no slot.

props.name: variant
props.description: Controls which CSS class is applied. primary = yellow CTA. secondary = ghost, dark surface only.

props.name: href
props.description: If provided, renders <a href={href}>. Without href, renders <button>. Do not pass href and also add role='button' — pick one element type.

props.name: class
props.description: Appended to the generated class string after the variant class. Never replaces it.

props.name: ...rest
props.description: Spread onto the root element. Use for onClick, data-*, disabled, aria-*, type attributes.

variants.description: Yellow background (--bg-accent), black text (--color-black), yellow glow shadow. Lifts on hover (translateY -2px, larger shadow). Presses on active (translateY +2px, no shadow). Works on any surface.

Transparent background, white text (--fg-on-dark-primary), glass border. Hover brightens border and adds subtle white fill. ONLY use on --bg-dark surfaces. White text is invisible on light backgrounds.

renderRule: href provided → renders as <a>. No href → renders as <button>. The prop alone controls element type — do not override with role attributes.
accessibility: label prop is the full accessible name — must be descriptive (not just 'Click here')
When rendered as <a>, href must be a real destination — not '#' or 'javascript:void(0)'
secondary variant uses white text; placing it on a light surface creates ~1:1 contrast — fails WCAG AA
Do not add disabled without also providing a visual indication — the disabled attribute removes it from tab order
whenToUse: primary: main calls-to-action anywhere on the site — hero CTAs, card CTAs, article footers
secondary: secondary actions placed directly on --bg-dark sections, typically alongside a primary button
whenNotToUse: secondary on light surfaces (--bg-primary, --bg-surface) or yellow surfaces (--bg-accent) — white text is invisible
For inline text navigation within a paragraph — use Link instead
For icon-only actions — label is required and must be text; use IconButton for icon-only
As a decorative element — Button always has interactive semantics (button or anchor role)

knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: Hero.astro is an Astro file — can import and use <Button variant='primary'>.
Hero.astro is an Astro file — can import and use <Button variant='secondary'>.

usageConstraints.reason: Button.astro cannot be imported into .jsx React islands (Header.jsx, Footer.jsx, WorkSection.jsx, TweaksPanel.jsx). Use raw class .hero-cta-primary / .hero-cta-secondary directly in JSX.
